title: Spicy Jamaican Mixed Spice Papillote
description: A flavorful and aromatic dish that combines the warmth of Jamaican mixed spice with the vibrant heat of scotch bonnet peppers, all wrapped up in a papillote for a delightful and easy meal. This spicy papillote is perfect for a quick weeknight dinner.
instructions: In a small bowl, mix together the Jamaican mixed spice, garlic, ginger, and scotch bonnet peppers.
In a large bowl, toss the chicken thighs with the spice mixture until coated.
In a separate bowl, mix together the flour and salt.
Dredge the chicken thighs in the flour mixture, shaking off excess.
Heat the coconut oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Sear the chicken thighs until browned on both sides, about 2-3 minutes per side.
Transfer the skillet to the preheated oven and cook for 15-20 minutes, or until the chicken is cooked through.
While the chicken is cooking, prepare the papillote by cutting four squares of parchment paper, each large enough to wrap around a chicken thigh.
To assemble the papillote, place a chicken thigh on each piece of parchment paper, then sprinkle with fresh cilantro and a squeeze of lime juice.
Fold the parchment paper over the chicken to form a packet, then twist the top to seal.
Return the papillote to the oven and cook for an additional 5-7 minutes, or until the chicken is heated through and the papillote is crispy.
Serve the chicken papillote hot, garnished with additional cilantro if desired.
